name: Filet Mignons on a Potato Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Peel the potatoes and cut them into slices about 1/8-inch thick. As the potatoes are sliced, drop them into a bowl of cold water to prevent discoloration.
3. Spread about 1 tsp of butter over the bottom of a large heavy skillet with a lid. Drain the potato slices and pat dry. Layer them in the skillet, sprinkling the layers with small pieces of butter (using all but 2 tbsp).
4. Cover the skillet closely and cook on top of the stove over moderate heat for about 20 minutes.
5. Uncover and transfer to the oven. Bake for another 20 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, sprinkle the steaks with brandy. Set aside.
7. Heat remaining butter in a saucepan, add mushrooms and thyme, and cook until wilted. Add broth and cook until reduced by about one-third. Add sour cream and olives. Remove from heat and set aside.
8. Drain the brandy from the steaks and reserve. Heat oil in a heavy skillet and add the steaks. Cook over high heat for about 3-4 minutes on each side (or longer if you prefer your steaks medium to medium-well). Remove and keep hot.
9. Add brandy to the skillet and cook, stirring to dissolve brown particles that cling to the pan. Stir in the sour cream sauce and heat through without boiling.
10. Turn potato cake onto a warmed serving dish. Arrange steaks on top and spoon a little sauce over them. Serve remaining sauce separately.
